title: leucaena esculenta subsp. collinsii
intro: Leucaena esculenta subsp. collinsii, also known as the Collins' leucaena or bean tree, is a plant species within the genus Leucaena. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and infection. The plant is rich in nutrients, including protein, fiber, and minerals, providing health benefits such as improved digestion, reduced cholesterol levels, and enhanced immune function. Traditional herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and poultices made from the leaves, bark, and roots of the plant.
